question_text: A block of wood has a mass of 500 g and a volume of 1000 cm³. Is this wood more or less dense than water (density of water = 1 g/cm³)?
correct_answer: Less dense. The wood has a density of 0.5 g/cm³, which is less than water's 1 g/cm³, so it will float.
explanation: To find the density of the wood, divide mass by volume: 500 g ÷ 1000 cm³ = 0.5 g/cm³. Since this is less than 1 g/cm³, the wood is less dense than water and will float.
